working_now: Lane boards for campaign and campus sides
Event board owned by Volunteer Manager
Independent campus pages bridged to same-lane campaign boards

campaign_manager.scope: Oversees both campaign-lane boards and campus-lane boards

assistant_campaign_manager.scope: Supports Campaign Manager across both campaign and campus boards

volunteer_manager.scope: Volunteer system; owns the Event Board

lanes.label: Social / Media
lanes.goal: Grow the campus and statewide network with clear, honest posts.

lanes.label: Events
lanes.goal: Plan welcoming gatherings that turn strangers into teammates.

lanes.label: Voter Registration
lanes.goal: Help peers register and stay ready to vote — separate from VCI and launch-team counts.

lanes.label: Outreach / Canvass
lanes.goal: Expand the network through relationships first — no pressure pitches.

lanes.label: Campus Coordination
lanes.goal: Coordinate local teams so every seat has a clear next step.

goals.title: Statewide youth network (16–24)
goals.explain: Build a social network of young people across Arkansas who can engage locally and stay connected after the meeting.

goals.title: Make politics feel welcoming
goals.explain: Gatherings should feel social and useful — not like a long lecture.

goals.title: Power of 5
goals.explain: Each person starts with five trusted relationships and one useful next ask.

goals.title: Foundation for future candidates
goals.explain: Practice coordinating now so young leaders can run later — without inventing appointments tonight.

goals.title: Local soft power
goals.explain: Presence and relationships influence local politics more than one viral post.

goals.title: Ballot and lawmaking readiness
goals.explain: Young people can matter on ballot initiatives and lawmaking when the network is real.

goals.title: Collective power
goals.explain: Change happens together — seats, follow-up, and honest counting.

drilldowns.title: Why the 2026–2028 cusp matters
drilldowns.summary: Near-term elections can still be shaped by organized young voters — if we start now.
bullets: Organization takes months; waiting until fall loses the summer window.
Seats, conversation, and registration habits compound.
This is not a guarantee — it is a chance with work attached.

drilldowns.title: Why Arkansas colleges are the opening
drilldowns.summary: Colleges concentrate peers who already share routines, friends, and calendars.
bullets: Campus is a natural team: same place, same pressures, shared schedule.
Steve’s point: campus organization at this scale is rare here — rare is still possible.
Each campus keeps its own page; lane leaders still report into the matching campaign board.

drilldowns.title: Why young voters matter
drilldowns.summary: Young voters are decisive when they show up together — and underrepresented when they don’t.
bullets: Rules and culture lag who the population actually is.
Registration and turnout are separate metrics — keep them labeled honestly.

drilldowns.title: Ballot initiatives and lawmaking
drilldowns.summary: Beyond candidate races, young networks can show up on ballot work and civic process.
bullets: Start with registration readiness and relationship networks.
Do not invent Field Plan doctrine on this page.
Follow published materials when they land.

drilldowns.title: After the election — lift each other
drilldowns.summary: Tonight’s seats are practice for a longer leadership pipeline.
bullets: Swing cycles matter — and so does who stays organized afterward.
Uplifting peers into future leadership starts with real roles and honest follow-up.
Interest tonight is not an appointment.

drilldowns.title: Every campus is different
drilldowns.summary: We give a shared lane structure; each campus fills it in its own way.
bullets: Use your college page for local calendar and teams.
Same-lane campaign boards stay aligned statewide.
